text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer who wants to create software that is easy to read and maintain. One of the most important principles is using clear naming conventions. Instead of naming a variable "x" or "temp," use descriptive names like "userAge" or "totalPrice." This makes your code much more understandable for others and for your future self. Another key practice is to break your code into small, focused functions. Each function should do only one thing, making it easier to test and reuse. By following these simple guidelines, you can significantly improve the maintainability of your projects.
